pre_registered_predictions.P1_gamma1_null: PASS: gamma=1 has no phase-1 terrain write, no adaptive gate change, and no later release difference.
pre_registered_predictions.P2_stress_adaptive: PASS: stress gamma=0 and gamma=0.25 show phase-1 CHSH terrain write, later adaptive positive sampled steps, and later release change.
pre_registered_predictions.P3_localization: PASS by strict criterion: normal/storage can show later adaptive CHSH activity, but without phase-1 CHSH terrain write they remain classified negative.
pre_registered_predictions.P4_replay_specificity: PASS: matched replay reproduces Arm3 release after replaying the same phase-1 write trace and aligned adaptive sampling stream.

safe_interpretation: Finite-shot sampled CHSH terrain memory can shift later adaptive measurement/readout gates and alter later reactor output in stress context. The matched replay shows the post-write adaptive dynamics follow the written terrain trace; quantum-specificity remains at the Bell-bound measurement/write/readout boundary, not in ordinary local population plumbing.
limitations: Not chemical realism or biological metabolism.
The adaptive loop still uses simulated quantum states and simulated finite-shot CHSH measurements.
Normal/storage later adaptive CHSH activity is not counted as positive without phase-1 Bell-excess terrain inscription.
Post-write adaptive dynamics are not claimed to be quantum-specific beyond the measurement/readout eligibility rule.
